title: tanacetum vulgare subsp. siculum
intro: Tanacetum vulgare subsp. siculum, also known as tansy, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region characterized by its fern-like foliage and bright yellow flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial in treating digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and insect repellent. The medicinal properties of tansy are attributed to its essential oils, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including indigestion, bloating, and fever, as well as to repel insects and improve digestion. Tansy can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile remedy for promoting overall well-being.
